heroHeadline: US-China Dual Tariff Shock: US 104% on China, China 84% on US; JPMorgan Recession Probability Hits 65% — Highest Since Pandemic
heroSubtitle: April 9: Trump imposed an additional 50% tariff on all Chinese goods (total ~104%), and China raised its retaliation to 84% on all US imports — the deepest bilateral trade barrier since the 1930s Smoot-Hawley era. JPMorgan raised its US recession probability to 65%; Moody's Analytics crossed the 50% threshold. The April 7 US-Iran two-week ceasefire had briefly provided relief — Dow surged 1,325 points (+2.85%), WTI oil crashed 16.4% to $94.41 (biggest single-day drop since April 2020) — but the ceasefire remains fragile with Lebanon strikes continuing. The FOMC faces a stagflation trap ahead of its April 29–30 meeting: core PCE at 3.06% prohibits rate cuts, while slowing growth demands stimulus. Cleveland Fed Nowcast projects March CPI at 3.25%.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Conference Board, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Yale Budget Lab, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, Cleveland Fed Inflation Nowcasting
